It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1941.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/216039|title=天神山古墳|language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=August 20, 2020}}</ref> It is the largest ''kofun'' in Gunma Prefecture and the 28th largest in the country. It is the only kofun with a length in excess of 200 meters in eastern Japan.. It is also sometimes referred to as the {{nihongo|'''Dantaiyama Kofun'''|男体山古墳|}}.
==Overview== The Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. The tumulus is located on a fill with an elevation of approximately 40 meters, located near the center of the city of Ōta in eastern Gunma Prefecture. There was a [[Shinto shrine]] dedicated to [[Tenjin (kami)|Tenjin]] located on the "neck" of the tumulus, from which the name of the ''kofun'' is derived. Although the site is truncated by Gunma Prefectural Route 2 (which skirts the posterior dome) and the [[Tōbu Railway]] [[Tōbu Koizumi Line]] tracks (which crosses the outer moat) the tumulus is in good preservation, with the inner moat intact. The tumulus has a total length of 210 meters, with both the circular portion and the anterior rectangular portion in three tiers, and is orientated to the southwest. The surface was originally covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and both cylindrical ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' and figurine ''haniwa'' (shaped as people and houses) have been recovered. From the design of these ''haniwa'', the date of construction of the tumulus can be estimated to have been from the early middle of the 5th century. The tomb was robbed in antiquity and the structure of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is unknown, but the exposed stone [[sarcophagus]] is of a design peculiar to the [[Kinai|Kansai region]] and indicates a strong connection with the Yamato kingdom. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Excavated artifacts are stored and displayed at the {{nihongo|'''Ōta City Nittasō History Museum'''|太田市立新田荘歴史資料館||}}.
Approximately 300 meters to the Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun is the [[Nyotaizan Kofun]], a large scallop-shaped (帆立貝形古墳) tumulus with has the same orientation and was built at roughly the same time. It has a separate National Historic Site designation. There is also a {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) tumulus to the north of the Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun, with a diameter of 36 meters and height of 3.2 meters, designated "Kofun A" on the diagram. The same types of ''haniwa'' as the Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun have been discovered an it is believed to be contemporary with that tumulus. Another tumulus, designated "B" on the diaphragm is to the southwest; however, it appears to be a much later construction.
The identity of the person this tumulus was constructed for is unknown, but from its size and prominence, and the presence of a stone sarcophagus, it is assumed to be that of a local king who was either an ally or an opponent to Yamato, a ''[[Kuni no miyatsuko]]'' of ancient [[Keno Province]], or even of a general sent by Yamato to rule over Keno. Whatever the case, the person who built this tomb must have controlled the entire Keno area, as subsequent tombs were on a smaller scale and are ground into clusters corresponding to later subdivisions of the province. The ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' mentions the "Keno clan", who were active in diplomacy with the kingdoms of the [[Korean Peninsula]] ad who participated in expeditions of the Yamato kingdom. The relationship between the Nyotaizan Kofun and the Tenjinyama Kofun in the northeast is not clear, but a pair of tumuli consisting of a keyhole-shaped tumulus and a scalloped tumulus have been found in other locations, such as the Saitobaru Kofun ([[Saito, Miyazaki]]).
;Total length: 210 meters ;Anterior rectangular portion: 116 meters wide x 12 meters high, 3-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 120 meter diameter x 16.5 meters high, 3-tier ;Inner moat: 36 meter width (near circular portion) to 24 meter width (bottom of rectangular portion) x 1.2 meters depth ;Outer moat: 6 to 17 meters width
The tumulus is about 15 minutes on foot from [[:en:Ōta Station (Gunma)|Ōta Station]] on the [[Tōbu Railway]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Gallery== <gallery> 750130 Ōta Tenjin-yama Kofun aerial.jpg|Aerial image 太田天神山古墳出土 水鳥形埴輪.JPG|Haniwa recovered from site Ōtatenjinnyama-kofun fukiishi.JPG|Fukiishi Ota-tenjinnyama-kofun baichou-A.JPG|Kofun "A" </gallery>

The tomb was robbed in antiquity and the structure of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is unknown, but the exposed stone [[sarcophagus]] is of a design peculiar to the [[:en:Kinai|Kansai region]] and indicates a strong connection with the Yamato kingdom. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Excavated artifacts are stored and displayed at the {{nihongo|'''Ōta City Nittasō History Museum'''|太田市立新田荘歴史資料館||}}.
Approximately 300 meters to the Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun is the [[Nyotaizan Kofun]], a large scallop-shaped (帆立貝形古墳) tumulus with has the same orientation and was built at roughly the same time. It has a separate National Historic Site designation. There is also a {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) tumulus to the north of the Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun, with a diameter of 36 meters and height of 3.2 meters, designated "Kofun A" on the diagram. The same types of ''haniwa'' as the Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun have been discovered an it is believed to be contemporary with that tumulus. Another tumulus, designated "B" on the diaphragm is to the southwest; however, it appears to be a much later construction.
The identity of the person this tumulus was constructed for is unknown, but from its size and prominence, and the presence of a stone sarcophagus, it is assumed to be that of a local king who was either an ally or an opponent to Yamato, a ''[[Kuni no miyatsuko]]'' of ancient [[:en:Keno Province|Keno Province]], or even of a general sent by Yamato to rule over Keno. Whatever the case, the person who built this tomb must have controlled the entire Keno area, as subsequent tombs were on a smaller scale and are ground into clusters corresponding to later subdivisions of the province. The ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' mentions the "Keno clan", who were active in diplomacy with the kingdoms of the [[Korean Peninsula]] ad who participated in expeditions of the Yamato kingdom. The relationship between the Nyotaizan Kofun and the Tenjinyama Kofun in the northeast is not clear, but a pair of tumuli consisting of a keyhole-shaped tumulus and a scalloped tumulus have been found in other locations, such as the Saitobaru Kofun ([[Saito, Miyazaki]]).
;Total length: 210 meters ;Anterior rectangular portion: 116 meters wide x 12 meters high, 3-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 120 meter diameter x 16.5 meters high, 3-tier ;Inner moat: 36 meter width (near circular portion) to 24 meter width (bottom of rectangular portion) x 1.2 meters depth ;Outer moat: 6 to 17 meters width
The tumulus is about 15 minutes on foot from [[:en:Ōta Station (Gunma)|Ōta Station]] on the [[Tōbu Railway]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name =ŌtaTenjinyama Kofun | native_name =太田天神山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Ōtatenjinnyama-kofun zenkei.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Gunma Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|17|30.04|N|139|23|27.69|E|region:JP-10_type:landmark|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Ōta, Gunma|Ōta, Gunma]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =early 5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (Park) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
{{nihongo|'''Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun'''|太田天神山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Uchigashimacho neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Ōta, Gunma|Ōta]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]] in the northern [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1941.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/216039|title=天神山古墳|language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=August 20, 2020}}</ref> It is the largest ''kofun'' in Gunma Prefecture and the 28th largest in the country. It is the only kofun with a length in excess of 200 meters in eastern Japan.. It is also sometimes referred to as the {{nihongo|'''Dantaiyama Kofun'''|男体山古墳|}}.
==Overview== The Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. The tumulus is located on a fill with an elevation of approximately 40 meters, located near the center of the city of Ōta in eastern Gunma Prefecture. There was a [[Shinto shrine]] dedicated to [[Tenjin (kami)|Tenjin]] located on the "neck" of the tumulus, from which the name of the ''kofun'' is derived. Although the site is truncated by Gunma Prefectural Route 2 (which skirts the posterior dome) and the [[Tōbu Railway]] [[Tōbu Koizumi Line]] tracks (which crosses the outer moat) the tumulus is in good preservation, with the inner moat intact. The tumulus has a total length of 210 meters, with both the circular portion and the anterior rectangular portion in three tiers, and is orientated to the southwest. The surface was originally covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and both cylindrical ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' and figurine ''haniwa'' (shaped as people and houses) have been recovered. From the design of these ''haniwa'', the date of construction of the tumulus can be estimated to have been from the early middle of the 5th century. The tomb was robbed in antiquity and the structure of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is unknown, but the exposed stone [[sarcophagus]] is of a design peculiar to the [[:en:Kinai|Kansai region]] and indicates a strong connection with the Yamato kingdom. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Excavated artifacts are stored and displayed at the {{nihongo|'''Ōta City Nittasō History Museum'''|太田市立新田荘歴史資料館||}}.
Approximately 300 meters to the Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun is the [[Nyotaizan Kofun]], a large scallop-shaped (帆立貝形古墳) tumulus with has the same orientation and was built at roughly the same time. It has a separate National Historic Site designation. There is also a {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) tumulus to the north of the Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun, with a diameter of 36 meters and height of 3.2 meters, designated "Kofun A" on the diagram. The same types of ''haniwa'' as the Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun have been discovered an it is believed to be contemporary with that tumulus. Another tumulus, designated "B" on the diaphragm is to the southwest; however, it appears to be a much later construction.
The identity of the person this tumulus was constructed for is unknown, but from its size and prominence, and the presence of a stone sarcophagus, it is assumed to be that of a local king who was either an ally or an opponent to Yamato, a ''[[Kuni no miyatsuko]]'' of ancient [[:en:Keno Province|Keno Province]], or even of a general sent by Yamato to rule over Keno. Whatever the case, the person who built this tomb must have controlled the entire Keno area, as subsequent tombs were on a smaller scale and are ground into clusters corresponding to later subdivisions of the province. The ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' mentions the "Keno clan", who were active in diplomacy with the kingdoms of the [[:en:Korea|Korea]] ad who participated in expeditions of the Yamato kingdom. The relationship between the Nyotaizan Kofun and the Tenjinyama Kofun in the northeast is not clear, but a pair of tumuli consisting of a keyhole-shaped tumulus and a scalloped tumulus have been found in other locations, such as the Saitobaru Kofun ([[Saito, Miyazaki]]).

The tumulus is about 15 minutes on foot from [[:en:Ōta Station (Gunma)|Ōta Station]] on the [[Tōbu Railway]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

The same types of ''haniwa'' as the Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun have been discovered an it is believed to be contemporary with that tumulus. Another tumulus, designated "B" on the diaphragm is to the southwest; however, it appears to be a much later construction.
The identity of the person this tumulus was constructed for is unknown, but from its size and prominence, and the presence of a stone sarcophagus, it is assumed to be that of a local king who was either an ally or an opponent to Yamato, a ''[[Kuni no miyatsuko]]'' of ancient [[:en:Keno Province|Keno Province]], or even of a general sent by Yamato to rule over Keno. Whatever the case, the person who built this tomb must have controlled the entire Keno area, as subsequent tombs were on a smaller scale and are ground into clusters corresponding to later subdivisions of the province. The ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' mentions the "Keno clan", who were active in diplomacy with the kingdoms of the [[:en:Korea|Korea]] ad who participated in expeditions of the Yamato kingdom. The relationship between the Nyotaizan Kofun and the Tenjinyama Kofun in the northeast is not clear, but a pair of tumuli consisting of a keyhole-shaped tumulus and a scalloped tumulus have been found in other locations, such as the Saitobaru Kofun ([[Saito, Miyazaki]]).

The tumulus is about 15 minutes on foot from [[:en:Ōta Station (Gunma)|Ōta Station]] on the [[Tōbu Railway]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

revision.text:
{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name =ŌtaTenjinyama Kofun | native_name =太田天神山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Ōtatenjinnyama-kofun zenkei.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Gunma Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|17|30.04|N|139|23|27.69|E|region:JP-10_type:landmark|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Ōta, Gunma|Ōta, Gunma]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =early 5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (Park) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
{{nihongo|'''Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun'''|太田天神山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Uchigashimacho neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Ōta, Gunma|Ōta]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]] in the northern [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1941.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/216039|title=天神山古墳|language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=August 20, 2020}}</ref> It is the largest ''kofun'' in Gunma Prefecture and the 28th largest in the country. It is the only kofun with a length in excess of 200 meters in eastern Japan.. It is also sometimes referred to as the {{nihongo|'''Dantaiyama Kofun'''|男体山古墳|}}.
==Overview== The Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. The tumulus is located on a fill with an elevation of approximately 40 meters, located near the center of the city of Ōta in eastern Gunma Prefecture. There was a [[Shinto shrine]] dedicated to [[Tenjin (kami)|Tenjin]] located on the "neck" of the tumulus, from which the name of the ''kofun'' is derived. Although the site is truncated by Gunma Prefectural Route 2 (which skirts the posterior dome) and the [[Tōbu Railway]] [[Tōbu Koizumi Line]] tracks (which crosses the outer moat) the tumulus is in good preservation, with the inner moat intact. The tumulus has a total length of 210 meters, with both the circular portion and the anterior rectangular portion in three tiers, and is orientated to the southwest. The surface was originally covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and both cylindrical ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' and figurine ''haniwa'' (shaped as people and houses) have been recovered. From the design of these ''haniwa'', the date of construction of the tumulus can be estimated to have been from the early middle of the 5th century. The tomb was robbed in antiquity and the structure of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is unknown, but the exposed stone [[sarcophagus]] is of a design peculiar to the [[:en:Kinai|Kansai region]] and indicates a strong connection with the Yamato kingdom. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Excavated artifacts are stored and displayed at the {{nihongo|'''Ōta City Nittasō History Museum'''|太田市立新田荘歴史資料館||}}.
Approximately 300 meters to the Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun is the [[Nyotaizan Kofun]], a large scallop-shaped (帆立貝形古墳) tumulus with has the same orientation and was built at roughly the same time. It has a separate National Historic Site designation. There is also a {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) tumulus to the north of the Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun, with a diameter of 36 meters and height of 3.2 meters, designated "Kofun A" on the diagram. The same types of ''haniwa'' as the Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun have been discovered an it is believed to be contemporary with that tumulus. Another tumulus, designated "B" on the diaphragm is to the southwest; however, it appears to be a much later construction.
The identity of the person this tumulus was constructed for is unknown, but from its size and prominence, and the presence of a stone sarcophagus, it is assumed to be that of a local king who was either an ally or an opponent to Yamato, a ''[[Kuni no miyatsuko]]'' of ancient [[:en:Keno Province|Keno Province]], or even of a general sent by Yamato to rule over Keno. Whatever the case, the person who built this tomb must have controlled the entire Keno area, as subsequent tombs were on a smaller scale and are ground into clusters corresponding to later subdivisions of the province. The ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' mentions the "Keno clan", who were active in diplomacy with the kingdoms of the [[:en:Korea|Korea]] ad who participated in expeditions of the Yamato kingdom. The relationship between the Nyotaizan Kofun and the Tenjinyama Kofun in the northeast is not clear, but a pair of tumuli consisting of a keyhole-shaped tumulus and a scalloped tumulus have been found in other locations, such as the Saitobaru Kofun ([[:en:Saito, Miyazaki|Saito, Miyazaki]]).
;Total length: 210 meters ;Anterior rectangular portion: 116 meters wide x 12 meters high, 3-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 120 meter diameter x 16.5 meters high, 3-tier ;Inner moat: 36 meter width (near circular portion) to 24 meter width (bottom of rectangular portion) x 1.2 meters depth ;Outer moat: 6 to 17 meters width
The tumulus is about 15 minutes on foot from [[:en:Ōta Station (Gunma)|Ōta Station]] on the [[Tōbu Railway]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Gallery== <gallery> 750130 Ōta Tenjin-yama Kofun aerial.jpg|Aerial image 太田天神山古墳出土 水鳥形埴輪.JPG|Haniwa recovered from site Ōtatenjinnyama-kofun fukiishi.JPG|Fukiishi Ota-tenjinnyama-kofun baichou-A.JPG|Kofun "A" </gallery>

The tumulus is about 15 minutes on foot from [[:en:Ōta Station (Gunma)|Ōta Station]] on the [[Tōbu Railway]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

revision.text:
{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name =ŌtaTenjinyama Kofun | native_name =太田天神山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Ōtatenjinnyama-kofun zenkei.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Gunma Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|17|30.04|N|139|23|27.69|E|region:JP-10_type:landmark|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Ōta, Gunma|Ōta, Gunma]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =early 5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (Park) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
{{nihongo|'''Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun'''|太田天神山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Uchigashimacho neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Ōta, Gunma|Ōta]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]] in the northern [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1941.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/216039|title=天神山古墳|language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=August 20, 2020}}</ref> It is the largest ''kofun'' in Gunma Prefecture and the 28th largest in the country. It is the only kofun with a length in excess of 200 meters in eastern Japan.. It is also sometimes referred to as the {{nihongo|'''Dantaiyama Kofun'''|男体山古墳|}}.
==Overview== The Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. The tumulus is located on a fill with an elevation of approximately 40 meters, located near the center of the city of Ōta in eastern Gunma Prefecture. There was a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] dedicated to [[:en:Tenjin (kami)|Tenjin]] located on the "neck" of the tumulus, from which the name of the ''kofun'' is derived. Although the site is truncated by Gunma Prefectural Route 2 (which skirts the posterior dome) and the [[Tōbu Railway]] [[Tōbu Koizumi Line]] tracks (which crosses the outer moat) the tumulus is in good preservation, with the inner moat intact. The tumulus has a total length of 210 meters, with both the circular portion and the anterior rectangular portion in three tiers, and is orientated to the southwest. The surface was originally covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and both cylindrical ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' and figurine ''haniwa'' (shaped as people and houses) have been recovered. From the design of these ''haniwa'', the date of construction of the tumulus can be estimated to have been from the early middle of the 5th century. The tomb was robbed in antiquity and the structure of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is unknown, but the exposed stone [[:en:Sarcophagus|Sarcophagus]] is of a design peculiar to the [[:en:Kinai|Kansai region]] and indicates a strong connection with the Yamato kingdom. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Excavated artifacts are stored and displayed at the {{nihongo|'''Ōta City Nittasō History Museum'''|太田市立新田荘歴史資料館||}}.
Approximately 300 meters to the Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun is the [[Nyotaizan Kofun]], a large scallop-shaped (帆立貝形古墳) tumulus with has the same orientation and was built at roughly the same time. It has a separate National Historic Site designation. There is also a {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) tumulus to the north of the Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun, with a diameter of 36 meters and height of 3.2 meters, designated "Kofun A" on the diagram. The same types of ''haniwa'' as the Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun have been discovered an it is believed to be contemporary with that tumulus. Another tumulus, designated "B" on the diaphragm is to the southwest; however, it appears to be a much later construction.
The identity of the person this tumulus was constructed for is unknown, but from its size and prominence, and the presence of a stone sarcophagus, it is assumed to be that of a local king who was either an ally or an opponent to Yamato, a ''[[Kuni no miyatsuko]]'' of ancient [[:en:Keno Province|Keno Province]], or even of a general sent by Yamato to rule over Keno. Whatever the case, the person who built this tomb must have controlled the entire Keno area, as subsequent tombs were on a smaller scale and are ground into clusters corresponding to later subdivisions of the province. The ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' mentions the "Keno clan", who were active in diplomacy with the kingdoms of the [[:en:Korea|Korea]] ad who participated in expeditions of the Yamato kingdom. The relationship between the Nyotaizan Kofun and the Tenjinyama Kofun in the northeast is not clear, but a pair of tumuli consisting of a keyhole-shaped tumulus and a scalloped tumulus have been found in other locations, such as the Saitobaru Kofun ([[:en:Saito, Miyazaki|Saito, Miyazaki]]).
;Total length: 210 meters ;Anterior rectangular portion: 116 meters wide x 12 meters high, 3-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 120 meter diameter x 16.5 meters high, 3-tier ;Inner moat: 36 meter width (near circular portion) to 24 meter width (bottom of rectangular portion) x 1.2 meters depth ;Outer moat: 6 to 17 meters width
The tumulus is about 15 minutes on foot from [[:en:Ōta Station (Gunma)|Ōta Station]] on the [[Tōbu Railway]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Gallery== <gallery> 750130 Ōta Tenjin-yama Kofun aerial.jpg|Aerial image 太田天神山古墳出土 水鳥形埴輪.JPG|Haniwa recovered from site Ōtatenjinnyama-kofun fukiishi.JPG|Fukiishi Ota-tenjinnyama-kofun baichou-A.JPG|Kofun "A" </gallery>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11444167]] Wikidata name: Ōta Tenjin-yama Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:40, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name =ŌtaTenjinyama Kofun | native_name =太田天神山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Ōtatenjinnyama-kofun zenkei.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Gunma Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|17|30.04|N|139|23|27.69|E|region:JP-10_type:landmark|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Ōta, Gunma|Ōta, Gunma]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =early 5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (Park) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
{{nihongo|'''Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun'''|太田天神山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Uchigashimacho neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Ōta, Gunma|Ōta]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]] in the northern [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1941.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/216039|title=天神山古墳|language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=August 20, 2020}}</ref> It is the largest ''kofun'' in Gunma Prefecture and the 28th largest in the country. It is the only kofun with a length in excess of 200 meters in eastern Japan.. It is also sometimes referred to as the {{nihongo|'''Dantaiyama Kofun'''|男体山古墳|}}.
==Overview== The Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. The tumulus is located on a fill with an elevation of approximately 40 meters, located near the center of the city of Ōta in eastern Gunma Prefecture. There was a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] dedicated to [[:en:Tenjin (kami)|Tenjin]] located on the "neck" of the tumulus, from which the name of the ''kofun'' is derived. Although the site is truncated by Gunma Prefectural Route 2 (which skirts the posterior dome) and the [[:en:Tōbu Railway|Tōbu Railway]] [[:en:Tōbu Koizumi Line|Tōbu Koizumi Line]] tracks (which crosses the outer moat) the tumulus is in good preservation, with the inner moat intact. The tumulus has a total length of 210 meters, with both the circular portion and the anterior rectangular portion in three tiers, and is orientated to the southwest. The surface was originally covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and both cylindrical ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' and figurine ''haniwa'' (shaped as people and houses) have been recovered. From the design of these ''haniwa'', the date of construction of the tumulus can be estimated to have been from the early middle of the 5th century. The tomb was robbed in antiquity and the structure of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is unknown, but the exposed stone [[:en:Sarcophagus|Sarcophagus]] is of a design peculiar to the [[:en:Kinai|Kansai region]] and indicates a strong connection with the Yamato kingdom. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Excavated artifacts are stored and displayed at the {{nihongo|'''Ōta City Nittasō History Museum'''|太田市立新田荘歴史資料館||}}.
Approximately 300 meters to the Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun is the [[Nyotaizan Kofun]], a large scallop-shaped (帆立貝形古墳) tumulus with has the same orientation and was built at roughly the same time. It has a separate National Historic Site designation. There is also a {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) tumulus to the north of the Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun, with a diameter of 36 meters and height of 3.2 meters, designated "Kofun A" on the diagram. The same types of ''haniwa'' as the Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun have been discovered an it is believed to be contemporary with that tumulus. Another tumulus, designated "B" on the diaphragm is to the southwest; however, it appears to be a much later construction.
The identity of the person this tumulus was constructed for is unknown, but from its size and prominence, and the presence of a stone sarcophagus, it is assumed to be that of a local king who was either an ally or an opponent to Yamato, a ''[[Kuni no miyatsuko]]'' of ancient [[:en:Keno Province|Keno Province]], or even of a general sent by Yamato to rule over Keno. Whatever the case, the person who built this tomb must have controlled the entire Keno area, as subsequent tombs were on a smaller scale and are ground into clusters corresponding to later subdivisions of the province. The ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' mentions the "Keno clan", who were active in diplomacy with the kingdoms of the [[:en:Korea|Korea]] ad who participated in expeditions of the Yamato kingdom. The relationship between the Nyotaizan Kofun and the Tenjinyama Kofun in the northeast is not clear, but a pair of tumuli consisting of a keyhole-shaped tumulus and a scalloped tumulus have been found in other locations, such as the Saitobaru Kofun ([[:en:Saito, Miyazaki|Saito, Miyazaki]]).
;Total length: 210 meters ;Anterior rectangular portion: 116 meters wide x 12 meters high, 3-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 120 meter diameter x 16.5 meters high, 3-tier ;Inner moat: 36 meter width (near circular portion) to 24 meter width (bottom of rectangular portion) x 1.2 meters depth ;Outer moat: 6 to 17 meters width
The tumulus is about 15 minutes on foot from [[:en:Ōta Station (Gunma)|Ōta Station]] on the [[:en:Tōbu Railway|Tōbu Railway]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Gallery== <gallery> 750130 Ōta Tenjin-yama Kofun aerial.jpg|Aerial image 太田天神山古墳出土 水鳥形埴輪.JPG|Haniwa recovered from site Ōtatenjinnyama-kofun fukiishi.JPG|Fukiishi Ota-tenjinnyama-kofun baichou-A.JPG|Kofun "A" </gallery>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11444167]] Wikidata name: Ōta Tenjin-yama Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:40, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name =ŌtaTenjinyama Kofun | native_name =太田天神山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Ōtatenjinnyama-kofun zenkei.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Gunma Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|36.291678|N|139.391025|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Ota|qid=Q386179}} | region = [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =early 5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (Park) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
{{nihongo|'''Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun'''|太田天神山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Uchigashimacho neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Ōta, Gunma|Ōta]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]] in the northern [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1941.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/216039|title=天神山古墳|language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=August 20, 2020}}</ref> It is the largest ''kofun'' in Gunma Prefecture and the 28th largest in the country. It is the only kofun with a length in excess of 200 meters in eastern Japan.. It is also sometimes referred to as the {{nihongo|'''Dantaiyama Kofun'''|男体山古墳|}}.
==Overview== The Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. The tumulus is located on a fill with an elevation of approximately 40 meters, located near the center of the city of Ōta in eastern Gunma Prefecture. There was a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] dedicated to [[:en:Tenjin (kami)|Tenjin]] located on the "neck" of the tumulus, from which the name of the ''kofun'' is derived. Although the site is truncated by Gunma Prefectural Route 2 (which skirts the posterior dome) and the [[:en:Tōbu Railway|Tōbu Railway]] [[:en:Tōbu Koizumi Line|Tōbu Koizumi Line]] tracks (which crosses the outer moat) the tumulus is in good preservation, with the inner moat intact. The tumulus has a total length of 210 meters, with both the circular portion and the anterior rectangular portion in three tiers, and is orientated to the southwest. The surface was originally covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and both cylindrical ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' and figurine ''haniwa'' (shaped as people and houses) have been recovered. From the design of these ''haniwa'', the date of construction of the tumulus can be estimated to have been from the early middle of the 5th century. The tomb was robbed in antiquity and the structure of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is unknown, but the exposed stone [[:en:Sarcophagus|Sarcophagus]] is of a design peculiar to the [[:en:Kinai|Kansai region]] and indicates a strong connection with the Yamato kingdom. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Excavated artifacts are stored and displayed at the {{nihongo|'''Ōta City Nittasō History Museum'''|太田市立新田荘歴史資料館||}}.
Approximately 300 meters to the Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun is the [[Nyotaizan Kofun]], a large scallop-shaped (帆立貝形古墳) tumulus with has the same orientation and was built at roughly the same time. It has a separate National Historic Site designation. There is also a {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) tumulus to the north of the Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun, with a diameter of 36 meters and height of 3.2 meters, designated "Kofun A" on the diagram. The same types of ''haniwa'' as the Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun have been discovered an it is believed to be contemporary with that tumulus. Another tumulus, designated "B" on the diaphragm is to the southwest; however, it appears to be a much later construction.
The identity of the person this tumulus was constructed for is unknown, but from its size and prominence, and the presence of a stone sarcophagus, it is assumed to be that of a local king who was either an ally or an opponent to Yamato, a ''[[Kuni no miyatsuko]]'' of ancient [[:en:Keno Province|Keno Province]], or even of a general sent by Yamato to rule over Keno. Whatever the case, the person who built this tomb must have controlled the entire Keno area, as subsequent tombs were on a smaller scale and are ground into clusters corresponding to later subdivisions of the province. The ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' mentions the "Keno clan", who were active in diplomacy with the kingdoms of the [[:en:Korea|Korea]] ad who participated in expeditions of the Yamato kingdom. The relationship between the Nyotaizan Kofun and the Tenjinyama Kofun in the northeast is not clear, but a pair of tumuli consisting of a keyhole-shaped tumulus and a scalloped tumulus have been found in other locations, such as the Saitobaru Kofun ([[:en:Saito, Miyazaki|Saito, Miyazaki]]).
;Total length: 210 meters ;Anterior rectangular portion: 116 meters wide x 12 meters high, 3-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 120 meter diameter x 16.5 meters high, 3-tier ;Inner moat: 36 meter width (near circular portion) to 24 meter width (bottom of rectangular portion) x 1.2 meters depth ;Outer moat: 6 to 17 meters width
The tumulus is about 15 minutes on foot from [[:en:Ōta Station (Gunma)|Ōta Station]] on the [[:en:Tōbu Railway|Tōbu Railway]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Gallery== <gallery> 750130 Ōta Tenjin-yama Kofun aerial.jpg|Aerial image 太田天神山古墳出土 水鳥形埴輪.JPG|Haniwa recovered from site Ōtatenjinnyama-kofun fukiishi.JPG|Fukiishi Ota-tenjinnyama-kofun baichou-A.JPG|Kofun "A" </gallery>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Ōta Tenjin-yama Kofun]][[en:Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun]][[ja:太田天神山古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11444167]] Wikidata name: Ōta Tenjin-yama Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:40, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name =ŌtaTenjinyama Kofun | native_name =太田天神山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Ōtatenjinnyama-kofun zenkei.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Gunma Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|36.291678|N|139.391025|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Ota|WD=Q386179}} | region = [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =early 5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (Park) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
{{nihongo|'''Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun'''|太田天神山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Uchigashimacho neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Ōta, Gunma|Ōta]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]] in the northern [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1941.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/216039|title=天神山古墳|language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=August 20, 2020}}</ref> It is the largest ''kofun'' in Gunma Prefecture and the 28th largest in the country. It is the only kofun with a length in excess of 200 meters in eastern Japan.. It is also sometimes referred to as the {{nihongo|'''Dantaiyama Kofun'''|男体山古墳|}}.
==Overview== The Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. The tumulus is located on a fill with an elevation of approximately 40 meters, located near the center of the city of Ōta in eastern Gunma Prefecture. There was a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] dedicated to [[:en:Tenjin (kami)|Tenjin]] located on the "neck" of the tumulus, from which the name of the ''kofun'' is derived. Although the site is truncated by Gunma Prefectural Route 2 (which skirts the posterior dome) and the [[:en:Tōbu Railway|Tōbu Railway]] [[:en:Tōbu Koizumi Line|Tōbu Koizumi Line]] tracks (which crosses the outer moat) the tumulus is in good preservation, with the inner moat intact. The tumulus has a total length of 210 meters, with both the circular portion and the anterior rectangular portion in three tiers, and is orientated to the southwest. The surface was originally covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and both cylindrical ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' and figurine ''haniwa'' (shaped as people and houses) have been recovered. From the design of these ''haniwa'', the date of construction of the tumulus can be estimated to have been from the early middle of the 5th century. The tomb was robbed in antiquity and the structure of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is unknown, but the exposed stone [[:en:Sarcophagus|Sarcophagus]] is of a design peculiar to the [[:en:Kinai|Kansai region]] and indicates a strong connection with the Yamato kingdom. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Excavated artifacts are stored and displayed at the {{nihongo|'''Ōta City Nittasō History Museum'''|太田市立新田荘歴史資料館||}}.
Approximately 300 meters to the Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun is the [[Nyotaizan Kofun]], a large scallop-shaped (帆立貝形古墳) tumulus with has the same orientation and was built at roughly the same time. It has a separate National Historic Site designation. There is also a {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) tumulus to the north of the Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun, with a diameter of 36 meters and height of 3.2 meters, designated "Kofun A" on the diagram. The same types of ''haniwa'' as the Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun have been discovered an it is believed to be contemporary with that tumulus. Another tumulus, designated "B" on the diaphragm is to the southwest; however, it appears to be a much later construction.
The identity of the person this tumulus was constructed for is unknown, but from its size and prominence, and the presence of a stone sarcophagus, it is assumed to be that of a local king who was either an ally or an opponent to Yamato, a ''[[Kuni no miyatsuko]]'' of ancient [[:en:Keno Province|Keno Province]], or even of a general sent by Yamato to rule over Keno. Whatever the case, the person who built this tomb must have controlled the entire Keno area, as subsequent tombs were on a smaller scale and are ground into clusters corresponding to later subdivisions of the province. The ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' mentions the "Keno clan", who were active in diplomacy with the kingdoms of the [[:en:Korea|Korea]] ad who participated in expeditions of the Yamato kingdom. The relationship between the Nyotaizan Kofun and the Tenjinyama Kofun in the northeast is not clear, but a pair of tumuli consisting of a keyhole-shaped tumulus and a scalloped tumulus have been found in other locations, such as the Saitobaru Kofun ([[:en:Saito, Miyazaki|Saito, Miyazaki]]).
;Total length: 210 meters ;Anterior rectangular portion: 116 meters wide x 12 meters high, 3-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 120 meter diameter x 16.5 meters high, 3-tier ;Inner moat: 36 meter width (near circular portion) to 24 meter width (bottom of rectangular portion) x 1.2 meters depth ;Outer moat: 6 to 17 meters width
The tumulus is about 15 minutes on foot from [[:en:Ōta Station (Gunma)|Ōta Station]] on the [[:en:Tōbu Railway|Tōbu Railway]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Gallery== <gallery> 750130 Ōta Tenjin-yama Kofun aerial.jpg|Aerial image 太田天神山古墳出土 水鳥形埴輪.JPG|Haniwa recovered from site Ōtatenjinnyama-kofun fukiishi.JPG|Fukiishi Ota-tenjinnyama-kofun baichou-A.JPG|Kofun "A" </gallery>

The tumulus is about 15 minutes on foot from [[:en:Ōta Station (Gunma)|Ōta Station]] on the [[:en:Tōbu Railway|Tōbu Railway]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Ōta Tenjin-yama Kofun]][[en:Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun]][[ja:太田天神山古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11444167]] Wikidata name: Ōta Tenjin-yama Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:40, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name =ŌtaTenjinyama Kofun | native_name =太田天神山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Ōtatenjinnyama-kofun zenkei.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Gunma Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|36.291678|N|139.391025|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Ota|WD=Q386179}} | region = [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =early 5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (Park) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
{{nihongo|'''Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun'''|太田天神山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Uchigashimacho neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Ōta, Gunma|Ōta]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]] in the northern [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1941.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/216039|title=天神山古墳|language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=August 20, 2020}}</ref> It is the largest ''kofun'' in Gunma Prefecture and the 28th largest in the country. It is the only kofun with a length in excess of 200 meters in eastern Japan.. It is also sometimes referred to as the {{nihongo|'''Dantaiyama Kofun'''|男体山古墳|}}.
==Overview== The Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. The tumulus is located on a fill with an elevation of approximately 40 meters, located near the center of the city of Ōta in eastern Gunma Prefecture. There was a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] dedicated to [[:en:Tenjin (kami)|Tenjin]] located on the "neck" of the tumulus, from which the name of the ''kofun'' is derived. Although the site is truncated by Gunma Prefectural Route 2 (which skirts the posterior dome) and the [[:en:Tōbu Railway|Tōbu Railway]] [[:en:Tōbu Koizumi Line|Tōbu Koizumi Line]] tracks (which crosses the outer moat) the tumulus is in good preservation, with the inner moat intact. The tumulus has a total length of 210 meters, with both the circular portion and the anterior rectangular portion in three tiers, and is orientated to the southwest. The surface was originally covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and both cylindrical ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' and figurine ''haniwa'' (shaped as people and houses) have been recovered. From the design of these ''haniwa'', the date of construction of the tumulus can be estimated to have been from the early middle of the 5th century. The tomb was robbed in antiquity and the structure of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is unknown, but the exposed stone [[:en:Sarcophagus|Sarcophagus]] is of a design peculiar to the [[:en:Kinai|Kansai region]] and indicates a strong connection with the Yamato kingdom. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Excavated artifacts are stored and displayed at the {{nihongo|'''Ōta City Nittasō History Museum'''|太田市立新田荘歴史資料館||}}.
Approximately 300 meters to the Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun is the [[Nyotaizan Kofun]], a large scallop-shaped (帆立貝形古墳) tumulus with has the same orientation and was built at roughly the same time. It has a separate National Historic Site designation. There is also a {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) tumulus to the north of the Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun, with a diameter of 36 meters and height of 3.2 meters, designated "Kofun A" on the diagram. The same types of ''haniwa'' as the Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun have been discovered an it is believed to be contemporary with that tumulus. Another tumulus, designated "B" on the diaphragm is to the southwest; however, it appears to be a much later construction.
The identity of the person this tumulus was constructed for is unknown, but from its size and prominence, and the presence of a stone sarcophagus, it is assumed to be that of a local king who was either an ally or an opponent to Yamato, a ''[[Kuni no miyatsuko]]'' of ancient [[:en:Keno Province|Keno Province]], or even of a general sent by Yamato to rule over Keno. Whatever the case, the person who built this tomb must have controlled the entire Keno area, as subsequent tombs were on a smaller scale and are ground into clusters corresponding to later subdivisions of the province. The ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' mentions the "Keno clan", who were active in diplomacy with the kingdoms of the [[:en:Korea|Korea]] ad who participated in expeditions of the Yamato kingdom. The relationship between the Nyotaizan Kofun and the Tenjinyama Kofun in the northeast is not clear, but a pair of tumuli consisting of a keyhole-shaped tumulus and a scalloped tumulus have been found in other locations, such as the Saitobaru Kofun ([[:en:Saito, Miyazaki|Saito, Miyazaki]]).
;Total length: 210 meters ;Anterior rectangular portion: 116 meters wide x 12 meters high, 3-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 120 meter diameter x 16.5 meters high, 3-tier ;Inner moat: 36 meter width (near circular portion) to 24 meter width (bottom of rectangular portion) x 1.2 meters depth ;Outer moat: 6 to 17 meters width
The tumulus is about 15 minutes on foot from [[:en:Ōta Station (Gunma)|Ōta Station]] on the [[:en:Tōbu Railway|Tōbu Railway]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Gallery== <gallery> 750130 Ōta Tenjin-yama Kofun aerial.jpg|Aerial image 太田天神山古墳出土 水鳥形埴輪.JPG|Haniwa recovered from site Ōtatenjinnyama-kofun fukiishi.JPG|Fukiishi Ota-tenjinnyama-kofun baichou-A.JPG|Kofun "A" </gallery>

Approximately 300 meters to the Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun is the [[Nyotaizan Kofun]], a large scallop-shaped (帆立貝形古墳) tumulus with has the same orientation and was built at roughly the same time. It has a separate National Historic Site designation. There is also a {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) tumulus to the north of the Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun, with a diameter of 36 meters and height of 3.2 meters, designated "Kofun A" on the diagram. The same types of ''haniwa'' as the Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun have been discovered an it is believed to be contemporary with that tumulus. Another tumulus, designated "B" on the diaphragm is to the southwest; however, it appears to be a much later construction.
The identity of the person this tumulus was constructed for is unknown, but from its size and prominence, and the presence of a stone sarcophagus, it is assumed to be that of a local king who was either an ally or an opponent to Yamato, a ''[[Kuni no miyatsuko]]'' of ancient [[:en:Keno Province|Keno Province]], or even of a general sent by Yamato to rule over Keno. Whatever the case, the person who built this tomb must have controlled the entire Keno area, as subsequent tombs were on a smaller scale and are ground into clusters corresponding to later subdivisions of the province. The ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' mentions the "Keno clan", who were active in diplomacy with the kingdoms of the [[:en:Korea|Korea]] ad who participated in expeditions of the Yamato kingdom. The relationship between the Nyotaizan Kofun and the Tenjinyama Kofun in the northeast is not clear, but a pair of tumuli consisting of a keyhole-shaped tumulus and a scalloped tumulus have been found in other locations, such as the Saitobaru Kofun ([[:en:Saito, Miyazaki|Saito, Miyazaki]]).
;Total length: 210 meters ;Anterior rectangular portion: 116 meters wide x 12 meters high, 3-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 120 meter diameter x 16.5 meters high, 3-tier ;Inner moat: 36 meter width (near circular portion) to 24 meter width (bottom of rectangular portion) x 1.2 meters depth ;Outer moat: 6 to 17 meters width
The tumulus is about 15 minutes on foot from [[:en:Ōta Station (Gunma)|Ōta Station]] on the [[:en:Tōbu Railway|Tōbu Railway]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Ōta Tenjin-yama Kofun]][[en:Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun]][[ja:太田天神山古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11444167]] Wikidata name: Ōta Tenjin-yama Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:40, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name =ŌtaTenjinyama Kofun | native_name =太田天神山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Ōtatenjinnyama-kofun zenkei.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Gunma Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|36.291678|N|139.391025|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Ota|WD=Q386179}} | region = [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =early 5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (Park) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
{{nihongo|'''Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun'''|太田天神山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Uchigashimacho neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Ōta, Gunma|Ōta]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]] in the northern [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1941.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/216039|title=天神山古墳|language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=August 20, 2020}}</ref> It is the largest ''kofun'' in Gunma Prefecture and the 28th largest in the country. It is the only kofun with a length in excess of 200 meters in eastern Japan.. It is also sometimes referred to as the {{nihongo|'''Dantaiyama Kofun'''|男体山古墳|}}.
==Overview== The Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. The tumulus is located on a fill with an elevation of approximately 40 meters, located near the center of the city of Ōta in eastern Gunma Prefecture. There was a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] dedicated to [[:en:Tenjin (kami)|Tenjin]] located on the "neck" of the tumulus, from which the name of the ''kofun'' is derived. Although the site is truncated by Gunma Prefectural Route 2 (which skirts the posterior dome) and the [[:en:Tōbu Railway|Tōbu Railway]] [[:en:Tōbu Koizumi Line|Tōbu Koizumi Line]] tracks (which crosses the outer moat) the tumulus is in good preservation, with the inner moat intact. The tumulus has a total length of 210 meters, with both the circular portion and the anterior rectangular portion in three tiers, and is orientated to the southwest. The surface was originally covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and both cylindrical ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' and figurine ''haniwa'' (shaped as people and houses) have been recovered. From the design of these ''haniwa'', the date of construction of the tumulus can be estimated to have been from the early middle of the 5th century. The tomb was robbed in antiquity and the structure of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is unknown, but the exposed stone [[:en:Sarcophagus|Sarcophagus]] is of a design peculiar to the [[:en:Kinai|Kansai region]] and indicates a strong connection with the Yamato kingdom. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Excavated artifacts are stored and displayed at the {{nihongo|'''Ōta City Nittasō History Museum'''|太田市立新田荘歴史資料館||}}.
Approximately 300 meters to the Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun is the [[Nyotaizan Kofun]], a large scallop-shaped (帆立貝形古墳) tumulus with has the same orientation and was built at roughly the same time. It has a separate National Historic Site designation. There is also a {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) tumulus to the north of the Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun, with a diameter of 36 meters and height of 3.2 meters, designated "Kofun A" on the diagram. The same types of ''haniwa'' as the Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun have been discovered an it is believed to be contemporary with that tumulus. Another tumulus, designated "B" on the diaphragm is to the southwest; however, it appears to be a much later construction.
The identity of the person this tumulus was constructed for is unknown, but from its size and prominence, and the presence of a stone sarcophagus, it is assumed to be that of a local king who was either an ally or an opponent to Yamato, a ''[[Kuni no miyatsuko]]'' of ancient [[:en:Keno Province|Keno Province]], or even of a general sent by Yamato to rule over Keno. Whatever the case, the person who built this tomb must have controlled the entire Keno area, as subsequent tombs were on a smaller scale and are ground into clusters corresponding to later subdivisions of the province. The ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' mentions the "Keno clan", who were active in diplomacy with the kingdoms of the [[:en:Korea|Korea]] ad who participated in expeditions of the Yamato kingdom. The relationship between the Nyotaizan Kofun and the Tenjinyama Kofun in the northeast is not clear, but a pair of tumuli consisting of a keyhole-shaped tumulus and a scalloped tumulus have been found in other locations, such as the Saitobaru Kofun ([[:en:Saito, Miyazaki|Saito, Miyazaki]]).

The tumulus is about 15 minutes on foot from [[:en:Ōta Station (Gunma)|Ōta Station]] on the [[:en:Tōbu Railway|Tōbu Railway]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

The tumulus is located on a fill with an elevation of approximately 40 meters, located near the center of the city of Ōta in eastern Gunma Prefecture. There was a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] dedicated to [[:en:Tenjin (kami)|Tenjin]] located on the "neck" of the tumulus, from which the name of the ''kofun'' is derived. Although the site is truncated by Gunma Prefectural Route 2 (which skirts the posterior dome) and the [[:en:Tōbu Railway|Tōbu Railway]] [[:en:Tōbu Koizumi Line|Tōbu Koizumi Line]] tracks (which crosses the outer moat) the tumulus is in good preservation, with the inner moat intact. The tumulus has a total length of 210 meters, with both the circular portion and the anterior rectangular portion in three tiers, and is orientated to the southwest. The surface was originally covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and both cylindrical ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' and figurine ''haniwa'' (shaped as people and houses) have been recovered. From the design of these ''haniwa'', the date of construction of the tumulus can be estimated to have been from the early middle of the 5th century. The tomb was robbed in antiquity and the structure of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is unknown, but the exposed stone [[:en:Sarcophagus|Sarcophagus]] is of a design peculiar to the [[:en:Kinai|Kansai region]] and indicates a strong connection with the Yamato kingdom. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Excavated artifacts are stored and displayed at the {{nihongo|'''Ōta City Nittasō History Museum'''|太田市立新田荘歴史資料館||}}.
Approximately 300 meters to the Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun is the [[Nyotaizan Kofun]], a large scallop-shaped (帆立貝形古墳) tumulus with has the same orientation and was built at roughly the same time. It has a separate National Historic Site designation. There is also a {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) tumulus to the north of the Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun, with a diameter of 36 meters and height of 3.2 meters, designated "Kofun A" on the diagram. The same types of ''haniwa'' as the Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun have been discovered an it is believed to be contemporary with that tumulus. Another tumulus, designated "B" on the diaphragm is to the southwest; however, it appears to be a much later construction.
The identity of the person this tumulus was constructed for is unknown, but from its size and prominence, and the presence of a stone sarcophagus, it is assumed to be that of a local king who was either an ally or an opponent to Yamato, a ''[[Kuni no miyatsuko]]'' of ancient [[:en:Keno Province|Keno Province]], or even of a general sent by Yamato to rule over Keno. Whatever the case, the person who built this tomb must have controlled the entire Keno area, as subsequent tombs were on a smaller scale and are ground into clusters corresponding to later subdivisions of the province. The ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' mentions the "Keno clan", who were active in diplomacy with the kingdoms of the [[:en:Korea|Korea]] ad who participated in expeditions of the Yamato kingdom. The relationship between the Nyotaizan Kofun and the Tenjinyama Kofun in the northeast is not clear, but a pair of tumuli consisting of a keyhole-shaped tumulus and a scalloped tumulus have been found in other locations, such as the Saitobaru Kofun ([[:en:Saito, Miyazaki|Saito, Miyazaki]]).
;Total length: 210 meters ;Anterior rectangular portion: 116 meters wide x 12 meters high, 3-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 120 meter diameter x 16.5 meters high, 3-tier ;Inner moat: 36 meter width (near circular portion) to 24 meter width (bottom of rectangular portion) x 1.2 meters depth ;Outer moat: 6 to 17 meters width
The tumulus is about 15 minutes on foot from [[:en:Ōta Station (Gunma)|Ōta Station]] on the [[:en:Tōbu Railway|Tōbu Railway]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Gallery== <gallery> 750130 Ōta Tenjin-yama Kofun aerial.jpg|Aerial image 太田天神山古墳出土 水鳥形埴輪.JPG|Haniwa recovered from site Ōtatenjinnyama-kofun fukiishi.JPG|Fukiishi Ota-tenjinnyama-kofun baichou-A.JPG|Kofun "A" </gallery>

{{nihongo|'''Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun'''|太田天神山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Uchigashimacho neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Ōta, Gunma|Ōta]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]] in the northern [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1941.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/216039|title=天神山古墳|language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=August 20, 2020}}</ref> It is the largest ''kofun'' in Gunma Prefecture and the 28th largest in the country. It is the only kofun with a length in excess of 200 meters in eastern Japan.. It is also sometimes referred to as the {{nihongo|'''Dantaiyama Kofun'''|男体山古墳|}}.
==Overview== The Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. The tumulus is located on a fill with an elevation of approximately 40 meters, located near the center of the city of Ōta in eastern Gunma Prefecture. There was a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] dedicated to [[:en:Tenjin (kami)|Tenjin]] located on the "neck" of the tumulus, from which the name of the ''kofun'' is derived. Although the site is truncated by Gunma Prefectural Route 2 (which skirts the posterior dome) and the [[:en:Tōbu Railway|Tōbu Railway]] [[:en:Tōbu Koizumi Line|Tōbu Koizumi Line]] tracks (which crosses the outer moat) the tumulus is in good preservation, with the inner moat intact. The tumulus has a total length of 210 meters, with both the circular portion and the anterior rectangular portion in three tiers, and is orientated to the southwest. The surface was originally covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and both cylindrical ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' and figurine ''haniwa'' (shaped as people and houses) have been recovered. From the design of these ''haniwa'', the date of construction of the tumulus can be estimated to have been from the early middle of the 5th century. The tomb was robbed in antiquity and the structure of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is unknown, but the exposed stone [[:en:Sarcophagus|Sarcophagus]] is of a design peculiar to the [[:en:Kinai|Kansai region]] and indicates a strong connection with the Yamato kingdom. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Excavated artifacts are stored and displayed at the {{nihongo|'''Ōta City Nittasō History Museum'''|太田市立新田荘歴史資料館||}}.
Approximately 300 meters to the Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun is the [[Nyotaizan Kofun]], a large scallop-shaped (帆立貝形古墳) tumulus with has the same orientation and was built at roughly the same time. It has a separate National Historic Site designation. There is also a {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) tumulus to the north of the Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun, with a diameter of 36 meters and height of 3.2 meters, designated "Kofun A" on the diagram. The same types of ''haniwa'' as the Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun have been discovered an it is believed to be contemporary with that tumulus. Another tumulus, designated "B" on the diaphragm is to the southwest; however, it appears to be a much later construction.
The identity of the person this tumulus was constructed for is unknown, but from its size and prominence, and the presence of a stone sarcophagus, it is assumed to be that of a local king who was either an ally or an opponent to Yamato, a ''[[Kuni no miyatsuko]]'' of ancient [[:en:Keno Province|Keno Province]], or even of a general sent by Yamato to rule over Keno. Whatever the case, the person who built this tomb must have controlled the entire Keno area, as subsequent tombs were on a smaller scale and are ground into clusters corresponding to later subdivisions of the province. The ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' mentions the "Keno clan", who were active in diplomacy with the kingdoms of the [[:en:Korea|Korea]] ad who participated in expeditions of the Yamato kingdom. The relationship between the Nyotaizan Kofun and the Tenjinyama Kofun in the northeast is not clear, but a pair of tumuli consisting of a keyhole-shaped tumulus and a scalloped tumulus have been found in other locations, such as the Saitobaru Kofun ([[:en:Saito, Miyazaki|Saito, Miyazaki]]).

The tumulus is about 15 minutes on foot from [[:en:Ōta Station (Gunma)|Ōta Station]] on the [[:en:Tōbu Railway|Tōbu Railway]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
